display: Could you change compiletime_env.rs so that generics are shown with C and D? This would mean that functions would have a mangled type in this format: t_5C{parameter types and returned type, separated by “_”}DC{generic types, also separated by “_”}D. The function instances would then have this format: v_{variable ID}C{generic types of the instance}D. To be consistent with these two formats, functions that are not generic should have a “CD” in both its mangled type and name.

Could you change compiletime_env.rs so that generics are shown with C and D? This would mean that functions would have a mangled type in this format: t_5C{parameter types and returned type, separated by “_”}DC{generic types, also separated by “_”}D. The function instances would then have this format: v_{variable ID}s_0C{generic types of the instance}D. To be consistent with these two formats, functions that are not generic should have a “CD” in both its mangled type and name.

Could you add a "vec" type? This would need to work exactly like a "Vec" in Rust, just without the capital letter. This means that the transpiler would automatically generate the C code needed for all of the different use cases. To give you more detail, the vec type would be a pointer to an i32 (its size) and to an array of the vec's generic type (the values themselves). Since methods don't exist yet, there should be a push function, that takes in a "vec" (this would just copy the "vec", which is just a pointer), a clone function (which returns a new vec that points to a copy of the previous vec's values), and a pop function (which removes the last element). Get back to me when you have finished. You should also add tests for this.
